message:
fix(disk): refuse to materialize SQLite mailbox files in missing-DB probes; validate both repo_root + canonical_repo_root against symlinks

Three coupled defensive fixes around mailbox disk operations.

mcp-agent-mail-db/src/mailbox_verdict.rs (probe_schema_populated)
mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs (inspect_mailbox_db_inventory)
  Both helpers reach for `DbConn::open_file(<path>)` to do read-
  only diagnostics on the mailbox SQLite file.  But `open_file`
  passes `SQLITE_OPEN_CREATE` under the hood, which means calling
  it on a missing path silently materializes a zero-byte
  `.sqlite3` stub.  Subsequent `health_check`,
  `inspect_archive_drift`, and recovery flows then misclassify
  the mailbox as "present but empty" instead of "missing", and
  we leave behind a corrupted-looking artefact that the next
  agent has to clean up.

  Both helpers now check `db_path.exists()` (or `primary_path.exists()`)
  before calling `DbConn::open_file` and return a structured
  ProbeResult::error / SqlError::Custom in the missing case so
  callers can distinguish "no mailbox" from "broken mailbox"
  without side effects.

mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs (archive_repo_root_checked)
  `ProjectArchive` carries TWO root fields: `canonical_repo_root`
  (the pre-resolved form we validate at construction) and
  `repo_root` (a `pub` field external callers can mutate post-
  construction, e.g. shell installer migrations and re-target
  paths).  The prior `archive_repo_root_checked` only ran
  `reject_symlinked_archive_root` against `canonical_repo_root`,
  so a post-construction mutation that swung `repo_root` to a
  symlinked mount slipped past the symlink guard and then got
  used by archive operations downstream.  Now both fields are
  validated.

mcp-agent-mail-core/src/disk.rs
  Small `sqlite_path_component` refactor that drops a
  match-arm-shaped predicate in favour of a flatter conditional
  chain.  No behavioural change; included in this commit because
  it's in the same disk-operations layer as the above.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
